close_reason: Integration tests skip without env vars — correct CI behavior for credential-gated tests
description:
lib.rs:448-450 integration_enabled() returns false unless SECRETX_VAULT_TEST and VAULT_TOKEN are both set. Each integration test 'return's silently when integration_enabled() is false (lines 454-455, 475-476, 502-503, 517-518, 539-540, 584-585, 609-610, 411-413, 430-432).

Bad behavior: in CI without these env vars, these tests pass without running. A future regression that breaks (say) integration_field_put_drops_other_fields will not be detected unless someone notices the SECRETX_VAULT_TEST env var was missing. Tests-pass-without-running is a classic silent-failure mode.

Fix:
1. eprintln!('skipping <name> — set SECRETX_VAULT_TEST=1 to run') so the skip is visible in test output.
2. Better: gate them with #[ignore = "requires SECRETX_VAULT_TEST=1 and a live Vault"] so cargo test reports them as ignored rather than passed, and CI can run cargo test -- --ignored when the env is set.

P4 because all behaviors tested are also covered by the unit tests at the URI-parsing / validation layer; integration tests guard the wire-format glue, which is unlikely to silently regress.
title: Integration tests silently skip when env unset — no CI signal that they ran
